observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a) Municipal Accountant coordinates with the Municipal Engineering Office on the status of physical accomplishments of the foregoing infrastructure projects of the Municipality and to facilitate adjustments of the CIP and Inventory accounts equivalent to the amount reflective of their actual statuses; and
b) Accounting Office, Municipal Engineering Office, and Municipal Treasurer (as Supply Office), employ systems or procedures on the receipt and issuance of construction materials to aid proper documentation and recording to appropriate accounts thereof; and
c) Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant records the purchase of construction materials to the Construction Materials Inventory account in accordance with Section 114 of Volume I of the New Government Accounting System Manual and COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.reason: Some purchases of construction materials in CY 2024 were directly recorded to CIP and not adjusted as to the actual physical completion of projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management submit certified copies of contracts/Memoranda of Agreement indicating that funds were sourced out from the guaranty deposits and to ensure that investments are properly supported with detailed computation of the sources of funds prior to the undertaking, and that it would not result in cash deficit.
We also recommended that the Management adopt measures to recover the cash deficit, and to restore the amount of Trust Fund (TF) invested in treasury bonds and refrain from utilizing the said Fund other than the purpose for which it was established.
observations.reason: Computation of idle funds as available source of investment was not submitted to the Audit Team. In CY 2024, the Municipality made additional investment on Retail Treasury Bond worth ₱61.405 million.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Inventory Committee, Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer/Personnel In-Charge to:

a.	Complete the conduct the annual physical count of all the newly acquired Property, Plant and Equipment and update the RPCPPE to the current year; and
b.	Facilitate the completion of the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE), and the reconciliation of records thereof and its submission to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2022-005 dated February 22, 2022.

Recommendation

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2022-001 dated February 22, 2022.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact and in full daily or the next banking day to the authorized government depository bank consonant to Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 32 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2021-002 dated February 22, 2021.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
LDRRMO to assign tasks to job hire personnel related to the intended purpose to which the fund was established;
Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from the practice of charging expenses which are not related to the four thematic areas of LDRRM program/project/activities.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2021-002 dated February 22, 2021.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to consistently follow the procurement methods set in the APP and the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from paying through reimbursement when procuring goods and other services for the implementation of programs, projects, and activities especially those with pre-determined methods of procurement. Instead, the same should be paid directly to the suppliers who were identified as the lowest calculated and responsive supplier/bidder by the BAC following the prescribed procurement process and withhold the required taxes from related payments.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2021-004 dated February 22, 2021.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the MDRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to facilitate the review of the LGU’s long-term goal for the vehicles. He also shall direct the Municipal Accountant to review the subsidiary ledger of the Motor Vehicle Account and identify all those emergency vehicles that were distributed to the Barangays.
In case the ultimate goal is to let the Barangays utilize the vehicles for their BDRRM operations for the assets’ full life span with the corresponding operational expenses to be assumed by them, the Municipal Accountant should derecognize such assets in the LGU’s books with the proper documentation to support the recording of the transfer of the assets to each Barangay. Otherwise, the asset will remain in the Municipal book of accounts but reclassified as special account, say, Other Assets to be excluded from PPE.

The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to Management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2021-006 dated February 22, 2021.

Concerned officials and employees to stop the practice of paying through cash advances in the procurement of goods and other services for the implementation of municipal programs, projects, and activities. As to the unsettled accounts of former municipal employees and/or officials, the MM should exert more effort to recover the cash advance granted or cause their liquidation/settlement.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2016-008 dated February 28, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the assigned department head/s or personnel to fast track the submission of the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI), attached as “Annex IV” of the CY 2015 Annual Audit Report (attached herewith for ready reference), to the auditor concerned to facilitate monitoring and validations of the actions taken by the LGU on the related audit observations and recommendations, and eventually to submit a validation report to the Regional Director, COA Region VI, for monitoring purpose. Compliance on the submission of the same report to the above-mentioned oversight bodies should be made. Moreover, compliance is necessary to avoid further penalties as provided on this act.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2016-012 dated December 12, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strengthen the internal control system of the LGU especially in the disbursement process. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should see to it that all disbursement vouchers are duly supported with complete documentation to ensure the propriety, validity and legality of transactions.  The same (DVs) should not be handed over or left to the payees for considerable time but outright be returned to the treasury upon receipt of payment.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer facilitate the submission of the related documents of the above mentioned unbooked reconciling items to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2016-006 dated February 19, 2017

We recommend that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board strengthen its commitments in adopting a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management program. A regular meeting shall be made to keep abreast of the activities listed on their timeline so that necessary actions be made in areas where there is a need for improvement and to ensure that the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan will be implemented accordingly.
Furthermore, the LGU through the MSWMB should pursue a comprehensive disposal facility. Should it push through the use of the herein donated site, an agreement or donation shall be formally made between the municipality and the lot owner.
The aforementioned audit observation was communicated to management thru Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2016-003 dated February 7, 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation for the accounts which were not provided with depreciation. Prior years’ depreciation expenses should be recorded in the appropriate LGU’s books and should be charged against the Prior Year’s Adjustments account since they were no longer recorded in the years they were incurred. Subsequently, the Prior Year’s Adjustments should be closed to Government Equity.

Depreciation expenses should be recorded periodically in compliance with the above regulations and for the purpose of systematic and rational allocation of non-cash expenses over the assets’ useful lives. Proper recording and valuation of these assets would provide meaningful presentation of PPE accounts in the LGU’s financial statements.
